Resolves #9942

**What is the objective of this PR?**
- Isolates and truncates Sandman REM sleep Tuple memory extractions strictly within acceptable 4096 `n_ctx` boundaries for local `Qwen3-8B` MLX execution hardware.
- Hard limits payload variables natively to 3 execution tuples maximum or 4000 total byte allocation boundaries to provide adequate validation output buffer space.
- Formats explicit System vs User Prompt Array roles into `OpenAiCompatible` generate pipeline, preventing LLMs from failing output extraction requirements.

**Why are these changes required?**
DreamService processing dynamically concatenated tuples of Episodic string memories up into unescaped prompt matrices which often radically exceeded standard 4000 Token model thresholds for `Qwen3-8BMlx` hardware limits dynamically, creating native KV Buffer Crashes.

This update introduces structured `maxLen` payload clipping logic protecting the execution layer.

> ### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
> 
> *   **`[KB_GAP]`**: The explicit requirement decoupling `System` and `User` roles inside generic `prompt` string assignments wasn't apparent locally. Local inference frameworks (like MLX/LM Studio) will silently timeout generating continuation conversational structures if System Instructions are glued to the User document monolithicly.
> *   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: OpenAiCompatible stream TCP buffer natively swallowed JSON parse errors internally, masking the KV-cache overflow limits caused by payloads spanning beyond `4096` token `n_ctx` capabilities. We discovered this only by tracking execution timings that yielded 45s empty responses.
> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: The decision to truncate array structures strictly based on concluding turns (`slice(-3)`) dramatically improves output consistency for graph abstractions. Swarm intelligence organically places decision matrices at the final conversational outputs prior to memory persistence, meaning we retain ~100% of the strategic knowledge without exhausting contextual token windows!
>
